subject: GRB 190707B: Fermi GBM Final Real-time Localization
body:
The Fermi GBM team reports the detection of a likely LONG GRB

At 07:23:01 UT on 7 Jul 2019, the Fermi Gamma-ray Burst Monitor (GBM) triggered and located GRB 190707B (trigger 584176986.215968 / 190707308).

The on-ground calculated location, using the Fermi GBM trigger data, is RA = 184.3, Dec = -9.5 (J2000 degrees, equivalent to J2000 12h 17m, -9d 30'), with a statistical uncertainty of 4.9 degrees.

The angle from the Fermi LAT boresight is 72.0 degrees.

The skymap can be found here:
http://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2019/bn190707308/quicklook/glg_skymap_all_bn190707308.png

The HEALPix FITS file, including the estimated localization systematic, can be found here:
http://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2019/bn190707308/quicklook/glg_healpix_all_bn190707308.fit

The GBM light curve can be found here:
http://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2019/bn190707308/quicklook/glg_lc_medres34_bn190707308.gif
